questions.scenario: `devpolaris-orders-api` deployed successfully, but checkout fails only in production. The image digest matches the tested build. Which release piece should the team inspect next?

options.text: Runtime configuration and secret access for the production target.
The pull request title, because the artifact already proves runtime behavior.
The resource group description before checking app behavior.
A second deployment of the same artifact to clear the issue.

questions.explanation: A matching artifact proves which version was built, not that production settings, identity, and dependencies are correct.

questions.scenario: A release record is being prepared before traffic moves to a new Container Apps revision. Which items belong in the record?

options.text: The candidate revision and previous rollback revision.
The traffic percentage planned for the candidate.
The expected health and checkout signals for the watch window.
Only the engineer's local branch name.

questions.explanation: A release record should preserve runtime, traffic, health, and rollback evidence. A local branch name is not enough operating context.

questions.scenario: A teammate says the release is complete because the Azure deployment command exited successfully. What is the best response?

options.text: The deployment succeeded, but the release is complete only after runtime, config, traffic, health, and rollback evidence are checked.
The command output is the only reliable source because production traffic is noisy.
The team should ignore rollback unless an outage is already confirmed.
A successful deployment means configuration and identity were also validated.

questions.explanation: The article separates deployment success from release safety. A release includes the operating evidence around the deploy.
